name: Maple Pecan Cake
recipeInstructions:
1. Generously butter a 10-inch-diameter cake pan with high sides or a 7-1/2 x 11-1/2-inch cake pan with high sides, or an ovenproof glass baking dish.
2. In a heavy medium saucepan over low heat, heat the maple syrup, unsalted butter, brown sugar, and mace until the sugar dissolves. Bring to a boil.
3. Pour the mixture into the prepared pan and sprinkle with pecans.
4. Preheat the rack in the center of the oven to 350 degrees F (175 degrees C).
5. In a bowl, sift together flour, baking soda, and salt.
6. Cream 1/2 cup butter in another bowl until light and fluffy.
7. Add sugar and beat until light and fluffy. Beat in eggs one at a time, then beat for an additional 2 minutes.
8. Blend in vanilla and lemon peel.
9. Stir in the flour mixture alternately with yogurt, beginning and ending with the flour mixture. Fold in currants.
10. Spoon batter over the pecan mixture, spreading gently to the sides of the pan.
11. Bake until a tester inserted in the center comes out clean, about 55 minutes.
12. Immediately invert the cake onto a rack set over a sheet of waxed paper.
13. Serve warm. Pass whipped cream if desired.
